title: Minuartia regeliana
taxonomy: Minuartia regeliana is classified within the plant kingdom, specifically as a species of flowering plant. It belongs to the family Caryophyllaceae, commonly known as the pink or carnation family. The genus Minuartia is a group of small, annual or perennial plants found in the Northern Hemisphere. This species is further categorized as a member of the tribe Caryophylleae, which includes about 30 genera. Minuartia regeliana is also known by its synonym Arenaria regeliana.

intro: Minuartia regeliana, also known as Regel's sandwort, is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae, characterized by its small white or pink flowers and succulent leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits in treating conditions such as arthritis and improving cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Minuartia regeliana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
